args.report:
ADS Employment Offer Letter – Sara Khan, Data Analyst

[Attorney-Review Draft – Not for Signature]

This draft employment offer letter is prepared based on generic ADS fixed defaults and high-level Pakistan labour law considerations as understood for training purposes. It is not based on any specific ADS policy document or legal source, and no external authorities have been consulted in preparing this text. It must be reviewed, localised, and approved by a qualified attorney licensed in Pakistan (and any other relevant jurisdiction) before use.

---

[Analytics Dojo SMC Private Letterhead]

[Date: [BLANK]]

Sara Khan  
[Address Line 1: [BLANK]]  
[Address Line 2 / City / Postal Code: [BLANK]]  
[Email: [BLANK]]

Subject: Conditional Offer of Employment – Data Analyst

Dear Sara,

Analytics Dojo SMC Private ("ADS", "we", "us" or "the Company") is pleased to extend to you this conditional offer of employment for the position of Data Analyst (the "Position"), on the terms set out in this letter (the "Offer Letter"). This Offer Letter is subject to attorney review and to the satisfaction of the conditions described below.

1. Position and Reporting Line

1.1 Position. You will be employed in the role of Data Analyst on a full‑time basis, located in [City, Province: [BLANK]], Pakistan.

1.2 Reporting line. You will report to [Reporting Manager Title/Name: [BLANK]] or such other person as the Company may designate from time to time.

1.3 Duties. You will perform the duties and responsibilities customarily associated with the role of a data analyst in a technology / analytics company, including but not limited to data collection, cleaning, analysis, dashboarding, reporting, and collaboration with product and business stakeholders, together with any additional duties reasonably assigned to you from time to time, consistent with your Position and applicable law.

1.4 Work location and hours. Your primary place of work will be [Office Address / Remote Arrangement: [BLANK]]. Normal working hours will be [Standard Working Hours: [BLANK]] on [Working Days: [BLANK]], subject to Pakistan labour law limits on working time, overtime, rest breaks, and weekly rest.

2. Start Date and Term

2.1 Start date. Your anticipated start date will be [Start Date: [BLANK]] or such other date as we may mutually agree in writing.

2.2 Term. Your employment is intended to be ongoing and of indefinite duration, subject to the probationary period (if any), termination provisions, and applicable Pakistan labour law.

2.3 Probation. You will be subject to a probationary period of [Probation Length: [BLANK]] commencing on your Start Date, during which your performance and suitability for the Position will be assessed. Any probationary period and related termination rights will comply with applicable Pakistan labour law.

3. Compensation and Benefits

3.1 Base salary. You will receive a gross base salary of [PKR [BLANK] per month / per annum] (the "Base Salary"), payable in arrears in accordance with the Company’s standard payroll practices and subject to applicable tax and statutory deductions.

3.2 Variable compensation. You may be eligible for variable compensation (such as performance bonuses or incentives) in accordance with any applicable Company bonus or incentive plan as may be in effect from time to time. Any such plan will be discretionary, may be amended or withdrawn by the Company, and does not form part of your guaranteed compensation.

3.3 Statutory benefits. You will be entitled to all mandatory benefits required under applicable Pakistan labour and social security laws, which may include, as applicable based on your classification and tenure: contributions to social security / EOBI, group insurance where mandated, and any other statutory benefits in force from time to time.

3.4 Company benefits. In addition to statutory benefits, you may be eligible to participate in Company benefit plans and programs (if any) applicable to your grade and location, as may be introduced, amended, or withdrawn by the Company from time to time. Details of any such benefits will be provided separately.

3.5 Deductions. All payments to you will be subject to applicable tax withholding and other deductions required by law.

4. Working Time, Leave and Holidays

4.1 Working time. Your working hours, overtime (if any), and rest periods will comply with applicable Pakistan labour law, including any limits on daily and weekly working hours and requirements for weekly rest days.

4.2 Annual leave. You will be entitled to paid annual leave in accordance with applicable Pakistan labour law and the Company’s leave policy as in effect from time to time. The current indicative entitlement is [Annual Leave Days: [BLANK]] working days per year, accrued pro rata, subject to confirmation and legal review.

4.3 Public holidays and other leave. You will be entitled to public holidays and other statutory leave (such as sick leave, maternity/paternity leave, and other family or medical leave) in accordance with applicable Pakistan labour law. Any additional Company leave benefits will be as set out in the Company’s policies.

5. Confidentiality and Intellectual Property

5.1 Confidential information. During your employment, you will have access to confidential and proprietary information relating to ADS, its affiliates, clients, and partners. You must keep all such information strictly confidential, use it only for legitimate business purposes of the Company, and not disclose it to any third party except as authorised and required for your duties.

5.2 Continuing obligations. Your confidentiality obligations will continue both during and after your employment, in accordance with applicable law and any separate confidentiality or non‑disclosure agreement you may be asked to sign.

5.3 Intellectual property. Any intellectual property, including but not limited to software, code, models, algorithms, documentation, analyses, reports, dashboards, and other works, that you create, develop, or contribute to in the course of your employment and within the scope of your duties (or using Company resources) will, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, be the exclusive property of the Company. You agree to assign and hereby assign to the Company all such rights and to execute any documents reasonably required to perfect such rights, subject to Pakistan law.

6. Conduct, Policies and Data Protection

6.1 Company policies. You agree to comply with all Company policies, rules, and procedures as may be communicated to you from time to time, including but not limited to policies on code of conduct, anti‑harassment, anti‑discrimination, information security, data protection, and use of Company systems. These policies may be updated periodically and do not form part of this Offer Letter as contractual terms, except where expressly stated and permitted by law.

6.2 Data protection. The Company will process your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and its internal privacy policies, for purposes related to your employment and the operation of its business.

7. Termination

7.1 Notice. After completion of any probationary period, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving the other party not less than [Notice Period: [BLANK]] written notice or payment in lieu of notice, in accordance with applicable Pakistan labour law.

7.2 Termination during probation. During any probationary period, your employment may be terminated by either party on shorter notice, in accordance with applicable Pakistan labour law.

7.3 Summary termination. The Company may terminate your employment without notice or payment in lieu of notice in circumstances permitted by applicable Pakistan labour law, including but not limited to serious misconduct, gross negligence, or other grounds recognised by law.

7.4 Final settlement. Upon termination of your employment, you will be entitled to receive any salary, accrued but unused statutory leave, and other amounts due to you under applicable law and Company policy, subject to lawful deductions. Any statutory severance or end‑of‑service benefits will be provided where required by Pakistan labour law.

8. Outside Activities and Conflicts of Interest

8.1 Outside work. You must not, without the prior written consent of the Company, engage in any other employment, consultancy, or business activity that conflicts or may reasonably be expected to conflict with your duties to the Company or its legitimate business interests.

8.2 Conflicts of interest. You must promptly disclose to the Company any actual or potential conflict of interest of which you become aware.

9. Pakistan Labour Law and Local Compliance

9.1 Governing law. This Offer Letter and your employment relationship with the Company are intended to be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Pakistan, subject to mandatory local labour law protections that cannot be waived by contract.

9.2 Minimum standards. Nothing in this Offer Letter is intended to reduce or waive any minimum rights or protections you may have under applicable Pakistan labour law. In the event of any conflict between this Offer Letter and mandatory provisions of Pakistan labour law, the mandatory provisions will prevail.

9.3 Compliance table (high‑level, non‑exhaustive – for attorney review)

The following table is a high‑level checklist only. It is not legal advice and must be validated and completed by a qualified Pakistan employment lawyer before use.

| Topic                          | Indicative Treatment in this Offer Letter                          | To Be Confirmed by Pakistan Counsel |
|-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| Employment status             | Indefinite employment, with probation period                        | [ ]                                  |
| Working hours & overtime      | Normal hours + reference to statutory limits and rest               | [ ]                                  |
| Annual leave                  | Entitlement referenced; specific days left as [BLANK]               | [ ]                                  |
| Public holidays               | Statutory public holidays referenced                                | [ ]                                  |
| Sick / maternity / other leave| Statutory leave referenced                                          | [ ]                                  |
| Social security / EOBI        | Statutory contributions referenced                                  | [ ]                                  |
| Termination & notice          | Notice periods left as [BLANK]; statutory rights preserved          | [ ]                                  |
| Severance / end‑of‑service    | Statutory entitlements referenced                                   | [ ]                                  |
| IP and confidentiality        | Assignment and confidentiality clauses included, subject to law     | [ ]                                  |
| Non‑discrimination / conduct  | Reference to policies and legal compliance                          | [ ]                                  |

10. Entire Understanding and Variations

10.1 Entire understanding. This Offer Letter, together with any documents expressly referred to in it, sets out the principal terms of your proposed employment with the Company. It is not intended to be an exhaustive employment contract and may be supplemented by a more detailed employment agreement and/or Company policies, subject to applicable law.

10.2 Variations. Any variation to the terms of your employment must be in writing and signed by an authorised representative of the Company, subject always to compliance with applicable Pakistan labour law.

11. Conditions of this Offer

11.1 Conditions precedent. This offer of employment is conditional upon:

(a) your confirmation that you are legally entitled to work in Pakistan and, if applicable, the timely issuance and maintenance of any required work permits or approvals;  
(b) satisfactory completion of any background, reference, or credential checks that the Company may reasonably require, in compliance with applicable law; and  
(c) your agreement to sign any additional documents reasonably required by the Company in connection with your employment (such as a confidentiality or IP assignment agreement), subject to applicable law.

11.2 Withdrawal of offer. If any of the above conditions are not satisfied (or become incapable of being satisfied) before your Start Date, the Company may, subject to applicable law, withdraw or postpone this offer without liability.

12. Acceptance

If you wish to accept this conditional offer of employment on the terms set out in this Offer Letter, please sign and date the acknowledgement below and return a scanned copy to [Contact Email: [BLANK]] by [Acceptance Deadline: [BLANK]], or such other date as we may agree in writing.

This Offer Letter is issued subject to attorney review and is not intended to constitute legal advice. You are encouraged to seek independent legal advice regarding its terms and your rights under Pakistan labour law.

Yours sincerely,

For and on behalf of  
Analytics Dojo SMC Private


______________________________  
Name: [Authorised Signatory Name: [BLANK]]  
Title: [Authorised Signatory Title: [BLANK]]  
Date: [BLANK]


Acknowledged and agreed by:

I, Sara Khan, have read and understood this Offer Letter and accept the conditional offer of employment with Analytics Dojo SMC Private on the terms set out above, subject to my rights under applicable Pakistan labour law and subject to attorney review.


______________________________  
Sara Khan  
Date: [BLANK]
